new_classes.definition_en: A spirituous beverage obtained by simple or successive distillations of rectified cereal spirits or ethyl alcohol with juniper berries and other aromatic substances (e.g. coriander, angelica root, anise, ginger), classified under CN heading 2208 50 (Chapter 22).

new_classes.label_en: Gin in Containers of 2 Litres or Less (CN 22085011)
new_classes.label_de: Gin in Behältnissen mit einem Inhalt von 2 Litern oder weniger (KN 22085011)
new_classes.definition_en: Gin in containers holding 2 litres or less, classified under CN subheading 2208 50 11 (Chapter 22).

The process of aromatising rectified cereal spirits or ethyl alcohol with juniper berries (and optionally other aromatic substances such as coriander, angelica root, anise, or ginger) to produce gin; a subtype of distillation/flavouring process used to discriminate gin from other spirits in CN heading 2208 50.

coverage_explanation: The main distinguishing criteria covered are: (1) Gin is a spirit produced by distillation of rectified cereal spirits or ethyl alcohol with juniper berries and other aromatics — captured via the JuniperBerryAromatisation process restriction; (2) classification under CN 2208 50 — captured via cnHeadingCode hasValue "220850"; (3) the specific subheading 22085011 for containers ≤ 2 L — captured via the GinSmallContainer class with cnHeadingCode and maxContainerVolumeL restrictions. The legal note is truncated ("only spirituous beverag..."), so any additional eligibility conditions (e.g. minimum alcoholic strength, additional organoleptic requirements) mentioned in the full note could not be captured. Coverage is therefore approximately 0.82 rather than 1.0.
